name: ALSFRS-R
description: The ALS Functional Rating Scale - Revised (ALSFRS-R) is a validated instrument for assessing functional status in ALS patients. The questionnaire covers 12 areas of daily life across four domains.
introText: Please rate your current functional ability in the following areas. Choose the description that best matches your situation.

intro.title: Functional Assessment
intro.description: This questionnaire assesses your current functional ability in 12 areas of daily life. It takes about 5 minutes to complete.
intro.researchNote: Thank you for participating. Regular assessments help us better understand disease progression and advance ALS research.

3.label: Slight excess
3.description: Slight but definite excess saliva; may have nighttime drooling

2.label: Moderate excess
2.description: Moderately excessive saliva; may have minimal drooling

1.label: Marked excess
1.description: Marked excess saliva with some drooling

0.label: Severe excess
0.description: Marked drooling; requires constant tissue or handkerchief

0.label: Tube feeding only
0.description: Nothing by mouth; exclusively tube fed

3.label: Slow/Sloppy
3.description: Slow or sloppy; all words are legible

1.label: Can hold pen
1.description: Able to grip pen but unable to write

3.label: Slow/Clumsy
3.description: Somewhat slow and clumsy, but no help needed

2.label: Partial help needed
2.description: Can cut most foods, although clumsy and slow; some help needed

1.label: Needs to be fed
1.description: Food must be cut by someone, but can still feed slowly

3.label: Independent but slow
3.description: Independent and complete self-care with effort or decreased efficiency

turning_in_bed.text: Turning in bed and adjusting bed clothes

3.label: Slow/Clumsy
3.description: Somewhat slow and clumsy, but no help needed

2.label: Difficult alone
2.description: Can turn alone or adjust sheets, but with great difficulty

1.label: Help required
1.description: Can initiate, but not turn or adjust sheets alone

2.label: With light activity
2.description: Shortness of breath with one or more: eating, bathing, dressing

1.label: At rest
1.description: Shortness of breath at rest, difficulty breathing when sitting or lying

0.label: Significant
0.description: Significant difficulty, mechanical respiratory support considered

3.label: Slight problems
3.description: Some difficulty sleeping due to shortness of breath; does not routinely use more than 2 pillows

2.label: Extra pillows needed
2.description: Needs extra pillows to sleep (more than 2)

0.label: Unable to sleep
0.description: Unable to sleep without mechanical assistance

1.label: Continuous BiPAP
1.description: Continuous BiPAP use day and night

0.label: Invasive ventilation
0.description: Invasive mechanical ventilation by intubation or tracheostomy
